summary: Iter 7 — Razorpay webhook handler (POST /api/razorpay/webhook) fully tested. 13/13 webhook tests PASS, plus live 503 fail-closed verification when RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset. Regression suites (iter5 razorpay, iter6 coupons) still pass — the 3 failures observed during the combined run were transient Razorpay sandbox 401 ('api key expired' / 'Authentication failed') rate-limit blips unrelated to webhook code, and they passed on isolated re-run. .env has been restored (RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET removed; backend restarted; 503 reconfirmed).

minor.issue: Intermittent Razorpay 401 'Authentication failed' / 'api key expired' under bursty test load (rate-limit on rzp_test sandbox). Causes flaky failures in iter5 test_order_id_recognized_by_razorpay and iter6 test_checkout_uncouponed_full_price / test_already_enrolled_short_circuits when entire suite is hammered. Mitigation: add small sleep between checkout calls in tests, or cap parallelism. Not a webhook regression.

action_items: (Optional) Add inter-call throttling to checkout-heavy tests (iter5/iter6) so Razorpay sandbox rate limits don't cause flaky failures.
(Optional) Consider also accepting 'payment.authorized' alongside payment.captured (already in handler) — confirmed via code review.
critical_code_review_comments: Webhook handler correctly uses await request.body() for raw bytes BEFORE json parsing — signature is verified over EXACT bytes as sent.
hmac.compare_digest used (constant-time compare) for signature check — confirmed in /app/backend/server.py line 711.
Fail-closed: 503 'Webhook not configured' returned when RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is empty/unset — verified live.
_mark_paid_from_webhook uses {status:{'$ne':'paid'}} guard on update — ensures idempotency and no double coupon increment.
paid_via='webhook' is set on webhook-driven paid state (distinct from client /verify path, which does not set paid_via). Useful for audit.
Unknown event types and unknown order_ids correctly ack 200 so Razorpay stops retrying.
payment.failed only flips status when current status is 'pending' — won't overwrite a 'paid' enrollment.
/app/backend/server.py is 1203 lines — exceeds the 700-line recommendation. Suggest splitting into modules (auth, courses, coupons, enrollments, payments/webhook, admin) for maintainability.
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (temporarily added then removed RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET; restored to original)
test_credentials: Admin: admin@vishnuraghav.in / Admin@12345. Webhook secret used during test: test_webhook_secret_xyz_iter7 (now removed from .env).
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_wh_*/TEST_p1_*/TEST_p2_*/TEST_vfw_*/TEST_op_*/TEST_pf_*/TEST_rg_* student users + their pending/paid/failed enrollments. LAUNCH20 coupon used_count was incremented twice (once via webhook test, once via verify-first test) — left as-is.
context_for_next_testing_agent: POST /api/razorpay/webhook is live and battle-tested. To test again, set RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET in /app/backend/.env, restart backend (sleep 4), run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_webhook_iter7.py, then REMOVE the env var and restart again — otherwise iter5/iter6 are unaffected. Iter5/iter6 may flake on Razorpay sandbox 401s; re-run isolated to confirm. /app/backend/server.py is large (1203 lines) — flag for refactor.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature works end-to-end. Only minor flakiness on iter5/iter6 unrelated to this iteration; root cause is Razorpay sandbox rate-limit under burst load.
